title: Daily Market Digest: Markets Closed for Good Friday
subtitle: Friday, April 03, 2026

items.headline: U.S. Markets Closed for Good Friday; Cautious Optimism Prevails
items.summary: U.S. stock markets, including the Nasdaq and the New York Stock Exchange, are closed today, April 3, 2026, in observance of Good Friday. Overall market sentiment remains a blend of cautious optimism and volatility, as investors weigh recent economic data against ongoing geopolitical uncertainties, particularly the Iran conflict. Positive signals from the US labor market have provided a boost to the dollar, while oil prices saw a significant surge on Thursday.

items.statContext: S&P 500 futures slipped nearly 0.3% to 6,604.50. The index had eked out modest gains on Thursday. It is down 4.6% after Q1 2026.
The Nasdaq 100 index has entered a correction, down 10% from its all-time highs. It also saw modest gains on Thursday.
Dow futures were down 0.3% at 46,615.00. The Dow slipped on Thursday.

items.headline: Franklin Covey Leads April Gainers
items.summary: Franklin Covey Co. ($FC) has been the best performing stock in April 2026 so far.

items.headline: Plug Power Surges on Earnings
items.summary: Plug Power shares rose following strong fourth-quarter earnings reported on March 2nd, with shares still 25% higher a month later.

items.headline: Lipocine Inc. Plummets on Trial Failure
items.summary: Lipocine Inc. experienced a significant decline after its Phase 3 trial for a postpartum depression drug missed its primary endpoint.

items.headline: Hoth Therapeutics Declines on Offering
items.summary: Hoth Therapeutics, Inc. saw a substantial drop following the announcement of a $2.0 Million Registered Direct Offering.

items.headline: Tesla Shares Down on Q1 Deliveries
items.summary: Tesla reported that first-quarter deliveries and production rose year-on-year but fell from the prior quarter, sending shares down more than 5% on Thursday, April 2nd.

items.headline: U.S. Employment Situation: March Payrolls Up, Unemployment Stable
items.summary: The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics reported today that total nonfarm payroll employment increased by 178,000 in March 2026. The unemployment rate remained largely unchanged at 4.3 percent. Job gains were noted in health care, construction, and transportation and warehousing, while federal government employment continued its decline.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady, Future Cuts Uncertain
items.summary: The Federal Reserve held interest rates steady at its March meeting with an 11-1 vote, maintaining the federal funds rate in the 3.5% to 3.75% range. While some members forecast at least one additional rate cut this year, the possibility of another cut is not guaranteed, and one FOMC member even penciled in a rate hike for 2027.

items.headline: Energy, Materials, and Industrials Outperforming Tech in 2026
items.summary: So far in 2026, there has been a notable sector rotation, with Energy, Materials, and Industrials stocks significantly outperforming Technology. Energy stocks (XLE) are up 21.5%, Materials (XLB) are up 17.6%, and Industrials (XLI) have climbed 12.3%, while Technology stocks are down 3%. This shift is attributed to factors like 'AI fatigue' in tech, economic optimism, AI infrastructure buildout, and rising oil prices.

items.headline: Oil Prices Surge Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: U.S. oil futures recorded their biggest gain in six years on Thursday, with prices climbing more than $11 a barrel to close at $111.54. This surge is largely driven by ongoing worries of a prolonged Iran conflict, which continues to influence global markets.

items.headline: Banc of California to Release Q1 Earnings April 22nd
items.summary: Banc of California, Inc. ($BANC) announced it will release its financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, after market close on Wednesday, April 22, 2026. The company will host a conference call on April 23, 2026.
